- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

36	def _build_clusters(baskets: list[dict], ev_id_to_idx: dict[str, int]) -> list[SimpleNamespace]:
37	    clusters = []
38	    dropped = 0
39	    for b in baskets:
40	        if int(b.get("member_count", 1)) < 2:
41	            continue
42	        rep_id = b.get("representative_evidence_id")
43	        member_ids = list(b.get("member_evidence_ids") or [])
44	        if rep_id not in ev_id_to_idx or any(m not in ev_id_to_idx for m in member_ids):
45	            dropped += 1

47	        clusters.append(SimpleNamespace(
48	            representative_index=ev_id_to_idx[rep_id],

51	            member_hosts=list(b.get("member_hosts") or []),
52	        ))
53	    print(f"[bank] built {len(clusters)} clusters from {len(baskets)} baskets "
54	          f"(dropped {dropped} — id not resolvable in cp2 evidence)")
55	    return clusters

1	"""
2	POLARIS Query Amplifier
3	=======================
4	Generates 10x query variants for comprehensive SOTA-level search coverage.
5	
6	Key features:
7	- Academic variants (peer-reviewed, meta-analysis, systematic review)
8	- Regional variants (geographic targeting)
9	- Source-specific variants (site: operators)
10	- Temporal variants (recent, 2024-2026)
11	- Deduplication of generated variants
12	
13	Per CLAUDE.md LAW VI: Zero hard-coding. Uses DepthConfig for all parameters.
14	"""
15	
16	import logging
17	import os
18	from typing import List, Optional, Set
19	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
20	
21	try:
22	    from src.depth.depth_config import get_depth_config
23	except ImportError:
24	    get_depth_config = None  # Legacy module archived; polaris_graph uses its own query generation
25	
26	
27	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
28	
29	
30	# =============================================================================
31	# Constants for variant generation
32	# =============================================================================
33	
34	ACADEMIC_SUFFIXES = [
35	    "",  # Base query
36	    "peer-reviewed study",
37	    "systematic review",
38	    "meta-analysis",
39	    "clinical trial results",
40	    "research paper",
41	    "scientific literature",
42	    "evidence-based",
43	]
44	
45	REGIONAL_PATTERNS = {
46	    "NORTH_AMERICA": [
47	        "USA", "United States", "Canada", "North America",
48	        "American", "Canadian", "US federal",
49	    ],
50	    "EUROPE": [
51	        "Europe", "European Union", "EU", "UK", "United Kingdom",
52	        "Germany", "France", "European",
53	    ],
54	    "ASIA_PACIFIC": [
55	        "Asia", "China", "Japan", "Australia", "Asia Pacific",
56	        "India", "Southeast Asia", "APAC",
57	    ],
58	    "LATIN_AMERICA": [
59	        "Latin America", "South America", "Brazil", "Mexico",
60	        "Argentina", "Latin American",
61	    ],
62	    "GLOBAL": [
63	        "worldwide", "global", "international",
64	    ],
65	}
66	
67	GOVERNMENT_SITES = [
68	    "site:gov",
69	    "site:epa.gov",
70	    "site:fda.gov",
71	    "site:cdc.gov",
72	    "site:who.int",
73	    "site:nih.gov",
74	    "site:usda.gov",
75	]
76	
77	EDUCATIONAL_SITES = [
78	    "site:edu",
79	    "site:ac.uk",
80	    "site:edu.au",
81	]
82	
83	TEMPORAL_PATTERNS = [
84	    "2024 2025 2026",
85	    "latest research",
86	    "recent study",
87	    "current standards",
88	]

90	
91	# =============================================================================
92	# Query Amplifier
93	# =============================================================================
94	
95	@dataclass
96	class AmplificationResult:
97	    """Result of query amplification."""
98	    original_query: str
99	    variants: List[str]
100	    total_count: int
101	    dedup_count: int
102	    variant_types: List[str]
103	
104	
105	class QueryAmplifier:
106	    """
107	    Generates query variants for comprehensive SOTA-level search coverage.
108	
109	    Features:
110	    - Academic variants for research literature
111	    - Regional variants for geographic targeting
112	    - Source-specific variants (government, educational)
113	    - Temporal variants for recent research
114	    - Automatic deduplication
115	
116	    Usage:
117	        amplifier = QueryAmplifier()
118	        result = amplifier.amplify(
119	            query="household water filter contamination",
120	            region="NORTH_AMERICA",
121	            topic="water_quality"
122	        )
123	        print(f"Generated {result.total_count} variants")
124	    """
125	
126	    def __init__(self):
127	        """Initialize with DepthConfig (LAW VI)."""
128	        self.depth_config = get_depth_config()
129	        self.amplification_config = self.depth_config.query_amplification
130	
131	    def amplify(
132	        self,
133	        query: str,
134	        region: str = "GLOBAL",
135	        topic: Optional[str] = None,
136	        include_academic: Optional[bool] = None,
137	        include_regional: Optional[bool] = None,
138	        include_source: Optional[bool] = None,
139	        include_temporal: Optional[bool] = None,
140	    ) -> AmplificationResult:
141	        """
142	        Amplify a query into multiple variants.
143	
144	        Args:
145	            query: Original search query
146	            region: Geographic region (NORTH_AMERICA, EUROPE, etc.)
147	            topic: Optional topic category
148	            include_academic: Override academic variants setting
149	            include_regional: Override regional variants setting
150	            include_source: Override source variants setting
151	            include_temporal: Override temporal variants setting
152	
153	        Returns:
154	            AmplificationResult with all generated variants
155	        """
156	        if not self.amplification_config.enabled:
157	            return AmplificationResult(
158	                original_query=query,
159	                variants=[query],
160	                total_count=1,
161	                dedup_count=1,
162	                variant_types=["original"],
163	            )
164	
165	        variants: List[str] = []
166	        variant_types: List[str] = []
167	        seen: Set[str] = set()
168	
169	        # Always include original and exact match
170	        variants.append(query)
171	        variants.append(f'"{query}"')
172	        seen.add(query.lower())
173	        seen.add(f'"{query}"'.lower())
174	        variant_types.extend(["original", "exact_match"])
175	
176	        # Academic variants
177	        if include_academic if include_academic is not None else self.amplification_config.include_academic_variants:
178	            academic_variants = self._generate_academic_variants(query)
179	            for v in academic_variants:
180	                if v.lower() not in seen:
181	                    seen.add(v.lower())
182	                    variants.append(v)
183	                    variant_types.append("academic")
184	
185	        # Regional variants
186	        if include_regional if include_regional is not None else self.amplification_config.include_regional_variants:
187	            regional_variants = self._generate_regional_variants(query, region)
188	            for v in regional_variants:
189	                if v.lower() not in seen:
190	                    seen.add(v.lower())
191	                    variants.append(v)
192	                    variant_types.append("regional")
193	
194	        # Source-specific variants
195	        if include_source if include_source is not None else self.amplification_config.include_source_variants:
196	            source_variants = self._generate_source_variants(query)
197	            for v in source_variants:
198	                if v.lower() not in seen:
199	                    seen.add(v.lower())
200	                    variants.append(v)
201	                    variant_types.append("source")
202	
203	        # Temporal variants
204	        if include_temporal if include_temporal is not None else self.amplification_config.include_temporal_variants:
205	            temporal_variants = self._generate_temporal_variants(query)
206	            for v in temporal_variants:
207	                if v.lower() not in seen:
208	                    seen.add(v.lower())
209	                    variants.append(v)
210	                    variant_types.append("temporal")
211	
212	        # FIX-221C: Topic anchor enforcement
213	        # Ensure every variant contains at least one topic keyword to prevent drift
214	        topic_anchor_enabled = os.environ.get("POLARIS_QUERY_TOPIC_ANCHOR", "1") == "1"
215	        if topic_anchor_enabled and topic:
216	            # Extract topic anchor words from the original query
217	            topic_words = [w for w in query.split() if len(w) > 3 and w[0:1].isupper()]
218	            if not topic_words:
219	                # Fallback: use first 3 significant words from query
220	                _stop = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'in', 'on', 'at', 'for', 'to', 'of', 'and', 'or', 'with', 'by',
221	                         'what', 'how', 'which', 'where', 'when', 'why', 'do', 'does', 'exist', 'rates', 'patterns'}
222	                topic_words = [w for w in query.split() if w.lower() not in _stop][:3]
223	
224	            topic_anchor_str = ' '.join(topic_words[:3])
225	            topic_lower = {w.lower() for w in topic_words[:3]}
226	
227	            anchored_count = 0
228	            for i, v in enumerate(variants):
229	                if i == 0:
230	                    continue  # Skip original query
231	                v_lower = v.lower()
232	                has_topic = any(tw in v_lower for tw in topic_lower)
233	                if not has_topic:
234	                    # Prepend topic anchor
235	                    variants[i] = f"{topic_anchor_str} {v}"
236	                    anchored_count += 1
237	
238	            if anchored_count > 0:
239	                logger.info(
240	                    f"[FIX-221C] Anchored {anchored_count}/{len(variants)} variants "
241	                    f"with topic: '{topic_anchor_str}'"
242	                )
243	
244	        logger.info(
245	            f"Query amplification: '{query[:50]}...' -> {len(variants)} variants "
246	            f"(academic: {variant_types.count('academic')}, "
247	            f"regional: {variant_types.count('regional')}, "
248	            f"source: {variant_types.count('source')}, "
249	            f"temporal: {variant_types.count('temporal')})"
250	        )
251	
252	        return AmplificationResult(
253	            original_query=query,
254	            variants=variants,
255	            total_count=len(variants),
256	            dedup_count=len(variants),  # Already deduped
257	            variant_types=variant_types,
258	        )
259	
260	    def _generate_academic_variants(self, query: str) -> List[str]:
261	        """Generate academic/research variants."""
262	        variants = []
263	        for suffix in ACADEMIC_SUFFIXES:
264	            if suffix:
265	                variants.append(f"{query} {suffix}")
266	        return variants
267	
268	    def _generate_regional_variants(self, query: str, region: str) -> List[str]:
269	        """Generate regional/geographic variants."""
270	        variants = []
271	        patterns = REGIONAL_PATTERNS.get(region, REGIONAL_PATTERNS["GLOBAL"])
272	
273	        for pattern in patterns[:4]:  # Limit to 4 regional variants
274	            variants.append(f"{query} {pattern}")
275	            variants.append(f"{query} {pattern} statistics data")
276	
277	        # Add government report variant
278	        if region in ["NORTH_AMERICA", "EUROPE"]:
279	            variants.append(f"{query} {patterns[0]} government report")
280	            variants.append(f"{query} {patterns[0]} regulations standards")
281	
282	        return variants
283	
284	    def _generate_source_variants(self, query: str) -> List[str]:
285	        """Generate source-specific variants (site: operators)."""
286	        variants = []
287	
288	        # Government sources
289	        for site in GOVERNMENT_SITES[:4]:  # Limit to 4 government sites
290	            variants.append(f"{query} {site}")
291	
292	        # Educational sources
293	        for site in EDUCATIONAL_SITES[:2]:  # Limit to 2 educational sites
294	            variants.append(f"{query} {site}")
295	
296	        return variants
297	
298	    def _generate_temporal_variants(self, query: str) -> List[str]:
299	        """Generate temporal variants for recent research."""
300	        variants = []
301	        for pattern in TEMPORAL_PATTERNS:
302	            variants.append(f"{query} {pattern}")
303	        return variants
304	
305	    def amplify_batch(
306	        self,
307	        queries: List[str],
308	        region: str = "GLOBAL",
309	    ) -> List[str]:
310	        """
311	        Amplify multiple queries and return all unique variants.
312	
313	        Args:
314	            queries: List of original queries
315	            region: Geographic region
316	
317	        Returns:
318	            List of all unique query variants
319	        """
320	        all_variants: Set[str] = set()
321	
322	        for query in queries:
323	            result = self.amplify(query, region=region)
324	            all_variants.update(result.variants)
325	
326	        unique_variants = list(all_variants)
327	        logger.info(
328	            f"Batch amplification: {len(queries)} queries -> {len(unique_variants)} unique variants"
329	        )
330	
331	        return unique_variants
332	
333

335	# Standalone Functions

337	
338	def amplify_query(
339	    query: str,
340	    region: str = "GLOBAL",
341	    topic: Optional[str] = None,
342	) -> List[str]:
343	    """
344	    Standalone function to amplify a query.
345	
346	    Args:
347	        query: Original search query
348	        region: Geographic region
349	        topic: Optional topic category
350	
351	    Returns:
352	        List of query variants
353	    """
354	    amplifier = QueryAmplifier()
355	    result = amplifier.amplify(query, region=region, topic=topic)
356	    return result.variants
357	
358	
359	def amplify_queries(
360	    queries: List[str],
361	    region: str = "GLOBAL",
362	) -> List[str]:
363	    """
364	    Standalone function to amplify multiple queries.
365	
366	    Args:
367	        queries: List of original queries
368	        region: Geographic region
369	
370	    Returns:
371	        List of all unique query variants
372	    """
373	    amplifier = QueryAmplifier()
374	    return amplifier.amplify_batch(queries, region=region)
375	
376	
377	def count_amplification_factor() -> int:
378	    """
379	    Return the approximate amplification factor.
380	
381	    Returns:
382	        Approximate number of variants per query
383	    """
384	    config = get_depth_config()
385	    if not config.query_amplification.enabled:
386	        return 1
387	
388	    # Approximate: 2 (original) + 7 (academic) + 6 (regional) + 6 (source) + 4 (temporal)
389	    return config.query_amplification.variants_per_query

147	
148	IMPORTANT: Generate ANALYTICAL QUESTIONS, not keyword queries:
149	- WRONG: "water filter contamination study" (keyword query)
150	- RIGHT: "What health outcomes have been documented from contaminated water filters?" (analytical question)
151	
152	SUB-QUERY GUIDELINES:
153	1. Each sub-query should be specific and answerable
154	2. Assign clear data types (factual, statistical, comparative, procedural)
155	3. Prioritize: 1=critical for main question, 5=nice-to-have context
156	4. Include specific search keywords (3-5 per query)
157	5. Suggest authoritative domains when relevant
158	6. CRITICAL: Tag each query with its perspective_name
159	
160	SEARCH STRATEGIES:
161	- breadth_first: Start wide, gather many sources, then deep-dive (best for exploratory)
162	- depth_first: Start with key sources, follow citations (best for technical)
163	- hybrid: Parallel broad and deep searches (best for complex topics)
164	
165	SOURCE TYPES:
166	- academic: Peer-reviewed journals, systematic reviews
167	- government: CDC, EPA, FDA, regulatory agencies
168	- industry: Trade publications, manufacturer data
169	- news: Recent developments, case studies
170	- standards: ISO, ASTM, industry standards
171	
172	Create a comprehensive research plan that will THOROUGHLY address the question. Generate 20-30 sub-queries with concise keywords. CRITICAL: Always include search_strategy, key_entities, potential_sources, reasoning, AND perspective_name for each sub-query."""
173	
174	    def process(self, state: ResearchState) -> ResearchState:
175	        """
176	        Create research plan from query.
177	
178	        SOTA GAP-DRIVEN ITERATION:
179	        - First iteration: Generate comprehensive sub-queries from original query
180	        - Subsequent iterations: Use gaps from critic to generate TARGETED queries
181	
182	        Args:
183	            state: Current research state
184	
185	        Returns:
186	            Updated state with sub_queries, research_plan, search_strategy
187	        """
188	        query = state.get("original_query", "")
189	        vector_id = state.get("vector_id", "")
190	        query_type = state.get("query_type", "exploratory")
191	        complexity = state.get("complexity", "moderate")
192	        region = state.get("region", "GLOBAL")
193	        stage = state.get("stage", 1)
194	        iteration_count = state.get("iteration_count", 0)
195	
196	        if not query:
197	            raise ValueError("No query provided in state")
198	
199	        # ======================================================================
200	        # SOTA Integration: Multi-Language Query Handling (Task #21)
201	        # ======================================================================
202	        try:
203	            lang_handler = LanguageHandler()
204	            lang_result = lang_handler.detect_language(query)
205	
206	            state["query_language"] = {
207	                "detected": lang_result.detected_language.value,
208	                "confidence": lang_result.confidence,
209	                "is_english": lang_result.detected_language == Language.ENGLISH,
210	            }
211	
212	            if lang_result.detected_language != Language.ENGLISH and lang_result.confidence > 0.7:
213	                # Translate query to English for processing
214	                translation = lang_handler.translate_to_english(query, lang_result.detected_language)
215	                if translation.success:
216	                    logger.info(
217	                        f"[LANG] Translated query from {lang_result.detected_language.value} to English: "
218	                        f"'{query[:50]}...' -> '{translation.translated_text[:50]}...'"
219	                    )
220	                    # Store original and use translated for planning
221	                    state["original_query_language"] = lang_result.detected_language.value
222	                    state["original_query_native"] = query
223	                    query = translation.translated_text
224	                    state["original_query"] = query  # Update for downstream processing
225	            else:
226	                logger.debug(f"[LANG] Query language: {lang_result.detected_language.value} (confidence: {lang_result.confidence:.2f})")
227	        except Exception as e:
228	            logger.warning(f"[LANG] Language detection failed: {e}")
229	
230	        # SOTA FIX: Check if this is a gap-filling iteration
231	        gaps = state.get("gaps", [])
232	        gap_analysis = state.get("gap_analysis", {})
233	        iteration_feedback = state.get("iteration_feedback", "")
234	
235	        # If gaps exist from critic, use gap-driven planning
236	        if gaps and iteration_count > 0:
237	            return self._process_gap_driven(state, query, vector_id, gaps, gap_analysis, iteration_feedback)
238	
239	        # First iteration: Full planning from original query
240	        return self._process_initial_planning(state, query, vector_id, query_type, complexity, region, stage)

242	    def _process_gap_driven(
243	        self,
244	        state: ResearchState,
245	        query: str,
246	        vector_id: str,
247	        gaps: List,
248	        gap_analysis: dict,
249	        iteration_feedback: str
250	    ) -> ResearchState:
251	        """
252	        Gap-driven planning for iterations after the first.
253	
254	        SOTA PATTERN: Use suggested_queries from gaps instead of regenerating from scratch.
255	        This is how Gemini Deep Research, STORM, and Perplexity iterate.
256	        """
257	        # Collect suggested queries from gaps
258	        gap_queries = []
259	        for gap in gaps:
260	            # Handle both dict and Pydantic model
261	            if isinstance(gap, dict):
262	                suggested = gap.get("suggested_queries", [])
263	                gap_desc = gap.get("description", "")
264	                gap_priority = gap.get("priority", 3)
265	            else:
266	                suggested = getattr(gap, "suggested_queries", [])
267	                gap_desc = getattr(gap, "description", "")
268	                gap_priority = getattr(gap, "priority", 3)
269	
270	            for sq_text in suggested:
271	                gap_queries.append({
272	                    "query_text": sq_text,
273	                    "source_gap": gap_desc,
274	                    "priority": gap_priority
275	                })
276	
277	        # Also check prioritized_gaps from gap_analysis
278	        prioritized_gaps = gap_analysis.get("prioritized_gaps", [])
279	        for pg in prioritized_gaps[:5]:  # Top 5 prioritized gaps
280	            gap_desc = pg.get("description", "")
281	            # If no suggested queries, create one from description
282	            if gap_desc and not any(gq["source_gap"] == gap_desc for gq in gap_queries):
283	                gap_queries.append({
284	                    "query_text": f"{query} {gap_desc}",
285	                    "source_gap": gap_desc,
286	                    "priority": pg.get("priority", 3)
287	                })
288	
289	        if not gap_queries:
290	            # No gap queries found, fall back to LLM-based gap query generation
291	            logger.warning(f"No suggested_queries in gaps, using LLM to generate gap-filling queries")
292	            return self._generate_gap_queries_via_llm(state, query, vector_id, gaps, iteration_feedback)
293	
294	        # Convert gap queries to SubQuery objects
295	        sub_queries = []
296	        existing_ids = {sq.query_id for sq in state.get("sub_queries", []) if hasattr(sq, "query_id")}
297	
298	        for i, gq in enumerate(gap_queries):
299	            query_id = f"gap_{i+1:03d}"
300	            # Avoid duplicates
301	            if query_id in existing_ids:
302	                query_id = f"gap_{i+1:03d}_iter{state.get('iteration_count', 0)}"
303	
304	            # FIX-124: Gap-filling queries are tagged as "Gap_Filling" perspective
305	            sub_query = SubQuery(
306	                query_id=query_id,
307	                query_text=gq["query_text"],
308	                expected_data_type="factual",  # Gap-filling is usually factual
309	                priority=gq["priority"],
310	                search_keywords=gq["query_text"].split()[:10],  # Extract keywords
311	                domain_hints=[],
312	                perspective_name="Gap_Filling",  # FIX-124: STORM perspective for gap queries
313	                perspective_id=f"perspective_gap_{i+1:04d}",  # FIX-124: Unique gap perspective ID
314	                status="pending"
315	            )
316	            sub_queries.append(sub_query)
317	
318	        # APPEND to existing queries (don't replace)
319	        existing_queries = state.get("sub_queries", [])
320	        all_queries = list(existing_queries) + sub_queries
321	
322	        # Update state
323	        state["sub_queries"] = all_queries
324	        state["search_strategy"] = "depth_first"  # Gap-filling is depth-first
325	        state["research_plan"] = {
326	            "key_entities": state.get("research_plan", {}).get("key_entities", []),
327	            "potential_sources": state.get("research_plan", {}).get("potential_sources", []),
328	            "reasoning": f"Gap-driven iteration: targeting {len(sub_queries)} gaps identified by critic",
329	            "sub_query_count": len(all_queries),
330	            "gap_query_count": len(sub_queries),
331	            "iteration_type": "gap_driven"
332	        }
333	
334	        logger.info(
335	            f"Planner (GAP-DRIVEN): {vector_id} -> {len(sub_queries)} gap-filling queries "
336	            f"(total: {len(all_queries)}), gaps addressed: {len(gaps)}"
337	        )
338	
339	        return state

430	    def _validate_queries(
431	        self,
432	        original_query: str,
433	        sub_queries: List[SubQuery],
434	        vector_id: str = ""
435	    ) -> List[SubQuery]:
436	        """FIX 46 + FIX-124C: Validate sub-queries against original research question.
437	
438	        Filters out queries that are off-topic or unlikely to contribute
439	        to answering the original research question.
440	
441	        Uses:
442	        1. FIX-124C: Topic anchoring (core terms from vector_id)
443	        2. FIX 46: Embedding similarity
444	        """
445	        import os
446	        if os.environ.get("POLARIS_SKIP_QUERY_VALIDATION", "0") == "1":
447	            return sub_queries
448	
449	        # FIX-124C: Extract core topic terms for anchoring validation
450	        core_terms = extract_core_topic_terms(vector_id) if vector_id else []
451	        if not core_terms:
452	            # Fallback to extracting from original query
453	            from src.utils.query_utils import extract_core_topic_from_query
454	            core_terms = extract_core_topic_from_query(original_query)
455	
456	        if core_terms:
457	            # First pass: filter by topic relevance
458	            topic_validated = []
459	            topic_rejected = 0
460	            for sq in sub_queries:
461	                is_relevant, score = validate_query_relevance(sq.query_text, core_terms)
462	                if is_relevant or score >= 0.1:  # Keep if any overlap
463	                    topic_validated.append(sq)
464	                else:
465	                    topic_rejected += 1
466	
467	            if topic_rejected > 0:
468	                logger.info(
469	                    f"[FIX-124C] Topic anchoring: rejected {topic_rejected}/{len(sub_queries)} off-topic queries "
470	                    f"(core terms: {core_terms[:3]})"
471	                )
472	            sub_queries = topic_validated if topic_validated else sub_queries  # Don't filter all
473	
474	        try:
475	            from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
476	            import numpy as np
477	
478	            # Lazy-load model
479	            if not hasattr(self, '_embed_model'):
480	                self._embed_model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')
481	                logger.info("[FIX 46] Loaded sentence-transformers for query validation")
482	
483	            # Encode original query
484	            query_embedding = self._embed_model.encode(original_query, convert_to_numpy=True)
485	            query_norm = query_embedding / np.linalg.norm(query_embedding)
486	
487	            # Encode sub-queries
488	            sub_query_texts = [sq.query_text for sq in sub_queries]
489	            sub_embeddings = self._embed_model.encode(sub_query_texts, convert_to_numpy=True)
490	            sub_norms = sub_embeddings / np.linalg.norm(sub_embeddings, axis=1, keepdims=True)
491	
492	            # Compute similarities
493	            similarities = np.dot(sub_norms, query_norm)
494	
495	            # Filter queries with similarity >= 0.3 (reasonably related)
496	            RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD = 0.3
497	            validated = []
498	            for i, sq in enumerate(sub_queries):
499	                if similarities[i] >= RELEVANCE_THRESHOLD:
500	                    validated.append(sq)
501	                else:
502	                    logger.debug(f"[FIX 46] Filtered off-topic query (sim={similarities[i]:.2f}): {sq.query_text[:50]}...")
503	
504	            # Ensure we keep at least 5 queries (avoid over-filtering)
505	            if len(validated) < 5 and len(sub_queries) >= 5:
506	                # Sort by similarity and keep top 5
507	                sorted_indices = np.argsort(similarities)[::-1]
508	                validated = [sub_queries[i] for i in sorted_indices[:max(5, len(validated))]]
509	                logger.warning(f"[FIX 46] Over-filtered, keeping top {len(validated)} queries by similarity")
510	
511	            return validated
512	
513	        except ImportError:
514	            logger.warning("[FIX 46] sentence-transformers not installed, skipping query validation")
515	            return sub_queries
516	        except Exception as e:
517	            logger.warning(f"[FIX 46] Query validation failed: {e}, returning all queries")
518	            return sub_queries

541	    def _process_initial_planning(
542	        self,
543	        state: ResearchState,
544	        query: str,
545	        vector_id: str,
546	        query_type: str,
547	        complexity: str,
548	        region: str,
549	        stage: int
550	    ) -> ResearchState:
551	        """
552	        Initial planning for first iteration - comprehensive query decomposition.
553	
554	        FIX 72: Now includes LTM context to avoid re-researching known topics.
555	        """
556	        # FIX 72: Build prior knowledge context from LTM
557	        prior_knowledge = ""
558	        ltm_stage_context = state.get("ltm_stage_context", [])
559	        ltm_global_context = state.get("ltm_global_context", [])
560	
561	        if ltm_stage_context or ltm_global_context:
562	            prior_knowledge = "\n## PRIOR KNOWLEDGE (Already researched - DO NOT re-research these topics):\n"
563	
564	            # Add stage-level context (same topic, same region)
565	            for i, doc in enumerate(ltm_stage_context[:5]):
566	                content = doc.get("text", doc.get("content", ""))[:300]
567	                if content:
568	                    prior_knowledge += f"  {i+1}. [Stage {stage}] {content}...\n"
569	
570	            # Add global context (cross-stage insights)
571	            for i, doc in enumerate(ltm_global_context[:3]):
572	                content = doc.get("content", doc.get("text", ""))[:200]
573	                if content:
574	                    prior_knowledge += f"  {i+6}. [Global] {content}...\n"
575	
576	            prior_knowledge += "\nFocus on GAPS in the above knowledge. Generate queries for what is NOT covered.\n"
577	            logger.info(
578	                f"[FIX 72] Planner received {len(ltm_stage_context)} stage + "
579	                f"{len(ltm_global_context)} global prior knowledge items"
580	            )
581	
582	        # Build context
583	        context = f"""
584	Research Question: {query}
585	
586	Context:
587	- Vector ID: {vector_id}
588	- Query Type: {query_type}
589	- Complexity: {complexity}
590	- Geographic Scope: {region}
591	- Research Stage: {stage}
592	{prior_knowledge}
593	Create a detailed research plan with sub-questions that will comprehensively answer this question.
594	If prior knowledge is provided, focus on GAPS and NEW angles not already covered.
595	"""
596	
597	        # Call LLM with structured output
598	        messages = [
599	            SystemMessage(content=self.get_system_prompt()),
600	            HumanMessage(content=context)
601	        ]
602	
603	        plan: ResearchPlan = self.call_llm_structured(messages, ResearchPlan)
604	
605	        # FIX 12: Handle None return from call_llm_structured (timeout or parse failure)
606	        if plan is None:

608	            return state
609	
610	        # Convert to SubQuery objects
611	        # FIX-124: Track perspective distribution for logging
612	        perspective_counts = {}
613	        sub_queries = []
614	        for i, sq in enumerate(plan.sub_queries):
615	            # FIX-124: Generate perspective ID from perspective name
616	            perspective_name = getattr(sq, 'perspective_name', 'General') or 'General'
617	            perspective_id = f"perspective_{hash(perspective_name) % 10000:04d}"
618	
619	            # Track perspective distribution
620	            perspective_counts[perspective_name] = perspective_counts.get(perspective_name, 0) + 1
621	
622	            sub_query = SubQuery(
623	                query_id=f"sq_{i+1:03d}",
624	                query_text=sq.query_text,
625	                expected_data_type=sq.expected_data_type,
626	                priority=sq.priority,
627	                search_keywords=sq.search_keywords,
628	                domain_hints=sq.domain_hints,
629	                perspective_name=perspective_name,  # FIX-124: STORM perspective
630	                perspective_id=perspective_id,  # FIX-124: STORM perspective ID
631	                status="pending"
632	            )
633	            sub_queries.append(sub_query)
634	
635	        # FIX-124: Log perspective distribution
636	        if perspective_counts:
637	            dist_str = ", ".join(f"{k}={v}" for k, v in sorted(perspective_counts.items()))
638	            logger.info(f"[FIX-124] Perspective distribution: {dist_str}")
639	
640	        # FIX 46 + FIX-124C: Query validation loop - filter out off-topic queries
641	        validated_queries = self._validate_queries(query, sub_queries, vector_id=vector_id)
642	        if len(validated_queries) < len(sub_queries):
643	            logger.info(
644	                f"[FIX 46] Query validation: {len(sub_queries)} -> {len(validated_queries)} queries "
645	                f"(filtered {len(sub_queries) - len(validated_queries)} off-topic)"
646	            )
647	            state["query_validation_stats"] = {
648	                "original_count": len(sub_queries),
649	                "validated_count": len(validated_queries),
650	                "filtered_count": len(sub_queries) - len(validated_queries)
651	            }
652	        sub_queries = validated_queries
653	
654	        # Update state
655	        state["sub_queries"] = sub_queries
656	        state["search_strategy"] = plan.search_strategy
657	        state["research_plan"] = {
658	            "key_entities": plan.key_entities,
659	            "potential_sources": plan.potential_sources,
660	            "reasoning": plan.reasoning,
661	            "sub_query_count": len(sub_queries),
662	            "iteration_type": "initial"
663	        }
664	
665	        logger.info(
666	            f"Planner (INITIAL): {vector_id} -> {len(sub_queries)} sub-queries, "
667	            f"strategy={plan.search_strategy}"
668	        )
669	
670	        return state

89	def _wait_for_rate_limit(api_name: str):
90	    """FIX 105C: Ensure minimum interval between requests to the same API.
91	
92	    This provides additional protection beyond semaphores by enforcing
93	    a minimum time gap between consecutive requests.
94	    """
95	    with _REQUEST_LOCK:
96	        min_interval = _MIN_REQUEST_INTERVAL.get(api_name, 0.2)
97	        last_time = _LAST_REQUEST_TIME.get(api_name, 0.0)
98	        elapsed = time.time() - last_time
99	
100	        if elapsed < min_interval:
101	            sleep_time = min_interval - elapsed
102	            logger.debug(f"[FIX 105C] Rate limit: sleeping {sleep_time:.2f}s before {api_name} request")
103	            time.sleep(sleep_time)
104	
105	        _LAST_REQUEST_TIME[api_name] = time.time()

142	def extract_domain_from_url(url: str) -> str:
143	    """
144	    Extract domain from URL for diversity tracking.
145	
146	    Args:

164	def enforce_domain_diversity(results: List[dict]) -> List[dict]:
165	    """
166	    Enforce domain diversity by capping results per domain.
167	
168	    SOTA FIX W1.2: Prevents single domain dominance (was 38% from one domain).
169	
170	    Args:
171	        results: List of search results with 'url' field
172	
173	    Returns:
174	        Filtered list with domain caps applied
175	    """
176	    if not _DIVERSITY_CONFIG.get("enabled", True):
177	        return results
178	
179	    max_per_domain = _DIVERSITY_CONFIG.get("max_results_per_domain", 10)
180	
181	    # Count results per domain
182	    domain_counts: dict = {}
183	    filtered_results = []
184	
185	    for r in results:
186	        url = r.get("url", "")
187	        domain = extract_domain_from_url(url)
188	
189	        if not domain:
190	            # Keep results without valid URLs (e.g., graph results)
191	            filtered_results.append(r)
192	            continue
193	
194	        current_count = domain_counts.get(domain, 0)
195	
196	        if current_count < max_per_domain:
197	            filtered_results.append(r)
198	            domain_counts[domain] = current_count + 1
199	        else:
200	            logger.debug(f"[DIVERSITY-CAP] Skipped result from {domain} (cap={max_per_domain})")
201	
202	    # Log diversity statistics
203	    unique_domains = len(domain_counts)
204	    total_results = len(results)
205	    kept_results = len(filtered_results)
206	    removed = total_results - kept_results
207	
208	    if removed > 0:
209	        logger.info(
210	            f"[DIVERSITY-ENFORCEMENT] Kept {kept_results}/{total_results} results "
211	            f"({unique_domains} unique domains, removed {removed} over-cap)"
212	        )
213	
214	    # Check for low diversity warning
215	    if _DIVERSITY_CONFIG.get("warn_on_low_diversity", True) and unique_domains > 0:
216	        max_domain_count = max(domain_counts.values()) if domain_counts else 0
217	        max_domain_pct = max_domain_count / kept_results if kept_results > 0 else 0
218	        low_diversity_threshold = _DIVERSITY_CONFIG.get("low_diversity_threshold", 0.25)
219	
220	        if max_domain_pct > low_diversity_threshold:
221	            max_domain = max(domain_counts, key=domain_counts.get)
222	            logger.warning(
223	                f"[DIVERSITY-WARNING] Single domain {max_domain} has {max_domain_pct:.1%} "
224	                f"of results (threshold: {low_diversity_threshold:.0%})"
225	            )
226	
227	    return filtered_results

272	def deduplicate_results_with_perspective_merge(results: List["SearchResult"]) -> List["SearchResult"]:
273	    """
274	    Deduplicate search results by URL, merging perspective attributions.
275	
276	    FIX-124B: When same URL found by multiple STORM perspectives,
277	    merge all perspectives into perspective_origins list instead of
278	    overwriting (which loses perspective attribution).
279	
280	    Args:
281	        results: List of SearchResult objects to deduplicate
282	
283	    Returns:
284	        List of unique SearchResult objects with merged perspective_origins
285	    """
286	    seen_urls: dict = {}
287	
288	    for result in results:
289	        url = result.url.lower().rstrip("/")
290	
291	        if url not in seen_urls:
292	            # First time seeing URL - ensure perspective_origins is initialized
293	            if result.perspective_origin and result.perspective_origin not in result.perspective_origins:
294	                result.perspective_origins = [result.perspective_origin] + list(result.perspective_origins)
295	            seen_urls[url] = result
296	        else:
297	            existing = seen_urls[url]
298	            # Merge perspective_origin into perspective_origins
299	            if result.perspective_origin and result.perspective_origin not in existing.perspective_origins:
300	                existing.perspective_origins.append(result.perspective_origin)
301	
302	            # Keep higher-scored result but preserve merged perspectives
303	            existing_score = existing.metadata.get("score", 0.0) if existing.metadata else 0.0
304	            new_score = result.metadata.get("score", 0.0) if result.metadata else 0.0
305	
306	            if new_score > existing_score:
307	                # Transfer merged perspectives to higher-scored result
308	                merged_perspectives = list(set(existing.perspective_origins))
309	                result.perspective_origins = merged_perspectives
310	                seen_urls[url] = result
311	
312	    # Log multi-perspective URLs
313	    multi_count = sum(1 for r in seen_urls.values() if len(r.perspective_origins) > 1)
314	    if multi_count > 0:
315	        logger.info(f"[FIX-124B] {multi_count} URLs found by multiple perspectives")
316	
317	    return list(seen_urls.values())

638	        if loop.is_running():
639	            # This path causes "Event loop is closed" errors
640	            import concurrent.futures
641	            with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor() as executor:

1006	def _optimize_ddg_query(query: str) -> str:
1007	    """
1008	    FIX-125/FIX-221A: Optimize query for DuckDuckGo with topic anchoring.
1009	
1010	    DDG optimizations:
1011	    - Shorter queries work better (max 6-8 key terms)
1012	    - Remove filler words that don't help ranking
1013	    - FIX-221A: ALWAYS preserve topic anchor words (application + region)
1014	
1015	    Args:
1016	        query: Original search query
1017	
1018	    Returns:
1019	        Optimized query for DuckDuckGo
1020	    """
1021	    query_words = query.replace('"', '').split()
1022	
1023	    if len(query_words) <= 8:
1024	        return query
1025	
1026	    stop_words = {
1027	        'the', 'a', 'an', 'in', 'on', 'at', 'for', 'to', 'of', 'and', 'or',
1028	        'with', 'by', 'from', 'as', 'is', 'are', 'was', 'were', 'be', 'been',
1029	        'being', 'have', 'has', 'had', 'do', 'does', 'did', 'will', 'would',
1030	        'could', 'should', 'may', 'might', 'must', 'shall', 'can', 'need',
1031	        'this', 'that', 'these', 'those', 'what', 'which', 'who', 'whom',
1032	        'how', 'when', 'where', 'why', 'all', 'each', 'every', 'both', 'few',
1033	        'more', 'most', 'other', 'some', 'such', 'no', 'nor', 'not', 'only',
1034	        'own', 'same', 'so', 'than', 'too', 'very', 'just', 'but', 'if',
1035	        'exist', 'rates', 'patterns', 'applications',
1036	    }
1037	
1038	    # FIX-221A: Extract topic anchor words (capitalized nouns + region)
1039	    topic_anchor_enabled = os.environ.get("POLARIS_QUERY_TOPIC_ANCHOR", "1") == "1"
1040	    if topic_anchor_enabled:
1041	        topic_anchor = []
1042	        region_words = []
1043	        for w in query_words:
1044	            if w.isupper() and len(w) > 2:
1045	                region_words.append(w.title())
1046	            elif w[0:1].isupper() and w.lower() not in stop_words:
1047	                topic_anchor.append(w)
1048	        anchor = topic_anchor[:4] + region_words[:2]
1049	        anchor_lower = {w.lower() for w in anchor}
1050	        significant = [w for w in query_words if w.lower() not in stop_words and w.lower() not in anchor_lower]
1051	        optimized = ' '.join(anchor + significant[:3])
1052	    else:
1053	        significant = [w for w in query_words if w.lower() not in stop_words]
1054	        optimized = ' '.join(significant[:6])
1055	
1056	    logger.debug(f"[FIX-221A] DDG query optimized: '{query[:40]}...' -> '{optimized}'")
1057	    return optimized
1058

1060	# I-perm-012 (#1206): warn ONCE per process when no DuckDuckGo package is
1061	# installed, instead of one ERROR line per search attempt (the dozens-of-
1062	# identical-ERROR-lines-per-run noise observed on the VM that obscured real
1063	# monitoring signals). DuckDuckGo renamed the package duckduckgo_search -> ddgs
1064	# in 2025; we accept either.
1065	_DDG_IMPORT_WARNED = False

1068	def _duckduckgo_search(query: str, max_results: int = 10, region: str = "us-en") -> List[dict]:
1069	    """
1070	    FIX-125: Enhanced fallback search using DuckDuckGo with optimization.
1071	
1072	    Improvements:
1073	    - Query simplification for better DDG ranking
1074	    - Region parameter for geographic targeting
1075	    - Safe search disabled for research mode
1076	
1077	    I-perm-012 (#1206): import the new ``ddgs`` package (the 2025 rename of
1078	    ``duckduckgo_search``) with a fallback to the legacy name. If NEITHER is
1079	    installed, log once per process and fail soft (Serper / OpenAlex / Semantic
1080	    Scholar remain active) — the prior code logged the ImportError on EVERY
1081	    attempt, flooding the VM logs.
1082	
1083	    Args:
1084	        query: Search query
1085	        max_results: Maximum results to return
1086	        region: DDG region code (e.g., 'us-en', 'uk-en', 'de-de')
1087	
1088	    Returns:
1089	        List of search results
1090	    """
1091	    global _DDG_IMPORT_WARNED
1092	    try:
1093	        from ddgs import DDGS  # new package name (2025 rename)
1094	    except ImportError:
1095	        try:
1096	            from duckduckgo_search import DDGS  # legacy package name
1097	        except ImportError:
1098	            if not _DDG_IMPORT_WARNED:
1099	                logger.warning(
1100	                    "DuckDuckGo backend unavailable: neither 'ddgs' nor "
1101	                    "'duckduckgo_search' is installed. Skipping DDG for this "
1102	                    "process (Serper / OpenAlex / Semantic Scholar still "
1103	                    "active). Install 'ddgs' to re-enable this discovery backend."
1104	                )
1105	                _DDG_IMPORT_WARNED = True
1106	            return []
1107	
1108	    try:
1109	        # FIX-125: Optimize query for DDG
1110	        optimized_query = _optimize_ddg_query(query)
1111	
1112	        # Both ``ddgs`` and the legacy ``duckduckgo_search`` expose
1113	        # ``DDGS().text(...)`` returning an iterable of {title, href, body}.
1114	        # Instantiate directly (the new ddgs DDGS is not guaranteed to be a
1115	        # context manager). The positional query + keyword max_results/region/
1116	        # safesearch are accepted by both signatures.
1117	        results = []
1118	        for r in DDGS().text(
1119	            optimized_query,
1120	            max_results=max_results,
1121	            region=region,
1122	            safesearch="off",  # Research mode - no filtering
1123	        ):
1124	            results.append({
1125	                "url": r.get("href", ""),
1126	                "title": r.get("title", ""),
1127	                "snippet": r.get("body", ""),
1128	                "score": 0.5,  # DuckDuckGo doesn't provide scores
1129	                "source_type": "web",
1130	            })
1131	
1132	        logger.info(f"[FIX-125] DuckDuckGo search: '{optimized_query[:50]}' -> {len(results)} results")
1133	        return results
1134	
1135	    except Exception as e:
1136	        logger.error(f"DuckDuckGo search failed: {e}")
1137	        return []

173	                    StopReason.MAX_ITERATIONS: ConvergenceReason.MAX_ITERATIONS,
174	                    StopReason.TIME_LIMIT: ConvergenceReason.MAX_TIME,
175	                }
176	                convergence_reason = reason_map.get(
177	                    stop_decision.reason,
178	                    ConvergenceReason.QUALITY_THRESHOLD
179	                )
180	                logger.info(
181	                    f"[O3 PARITY] SophisticatedStopper decided to STOP: "
182	                    f"reason={stop_decision.reason.value}, "
183	                    f"coverage={stop_decision.coverage_score:.2%}, "
184	                    f"reason_detail={stop_decision.reason_detail}"
185	                )
186	                return False, convergence_reason
187	            else:
188	                logger.info(
189	                    f"[O3 PARITY] SophisticatedStopper: CONTINUE "
190	                    f"(coverage={stop_decision.coverage_score:.2%})"
191	                )
192	        except Exception as e:
193	            logger.warning(f"[O3 PARITY] SophisticatedStopper error, falling back: {e}")
194	            # Fall through to legacy checks
195	
196	        # =====================================================================
197	        # LEGACY: Circuit breaker checks (kept as fallback)
198	        # =====================================================================
199	
200	        # 1. Max iterations
201	        if current_iteration >= self.config.max_iterations:
202	            logger.info(f"Max iterations ({self.config.max_iterations}) reached")
203	            return False, ConvergenceReason.MAX_ITERATIONS
204	
205	        # 2. Max execution time
206	        if elapsed >= self.config.max_execution_minutes:
207	            logger.info(f"Max execution time ({self.config.max_execution_minutes}m) reached")
208	            return False, ConvergenceReason.MAX_TIME
209	
210	        # 3. Minimum iterations not met
211	        if current_iteration < self.config.min_iterations:
212	            return True, None
213	
214	        # Check convergence conditions
215	
216	        # 4. Quality thresholds met
217	        # FIX 23 (Gemini Audit FIX 3): Prefer Auditor's measured faithfulness
218	        # (post_hoc_faithfulness) over Critic's estimate (quality_metrics.faithfulness).
219	        # Critic's score is an LLM guess (~0.5); Auditor's is a MiniCheck measurement.
220	        quality = state.get("quality_metrics", {})
221	        if isinstance(quality, dict):
222	            faithfulness = quality.get("faithfulness", 0)
223	            precision = quality.get("context_precision", 0)
224	            relevancy = quality.get("answer_relevancy", 0)
225	        else:
226	            faithfulness = getattr(quality, "faithfulness", 0)
227	            precision = getattr(quality, "context_precision", 0)
228	            relevancy = getattr(quality, "answer_relevancy", 0)
229	
230	        # FIX 23: Override Critic's faithfulness with Auditor's if available
231	        auditor_faithfulness = state.get("post_hoc_faithfulness", 0)
232	        if auditor_faithfulness > 0:
233	            logger.info(
234	                f"FIX 23: Using Auditor faithfulness ({auditor_faithfulness:.2f}) "
235	                f"over Critic estimate ({faithfulness:.2f})"
236	            )
237	            faithfulness = auditor_faithfulness
238	
239	        if (faithfulness >= self.config.min_faithfulness and
240	            precision >= self.config.min_context_precision and
241	            relevancy >= self.config.min_answer_relevancy):
242	            logger.info(f"Quality thresholds met: F={faithfulness:.2f}, P={precision:.2f}, R={relevancy:.2f}")
243	            return False, ConvergenceReason.QUALITY_THRESHOLD
244	
245	        # 5. Knowledge saturation
246	        if self.saturation.consecutive_low_novelty >= self.config.consecutive_low_novelty:
247	            logger.info(f"Knowledge saturation detected after {self.saturation.consecutive_low_novelty} low-novelty iterations")
248	            return False, ConvergenceReason.KNOWLEDGE_SATURATION
249	
250	        # 6. No gaps remaining
251	        gaps = state.get("identified_gaps", [])
252	        if not gaps and current_iteration >= self.config.min_iterations:
253	            logger.info("No gaps remaining to fill")
254	            return False, ConvergenceReason.NO_GAPS_REMAINING
255	
256	        # Continue iterating
257	        return True, None

259	    def update_saturation(
260	        self,
261	        state: Dict[str, Any],
262	        current_iteration: int
263	    ) -> float:
264	        """
265	        Update saturation state and calculate novelty score.
266	
267	        Args:
268	            state: Current research state
269	            current_iteration: Current iteration number
270	
271	        Returns:
272	            Novelty score for this iteration
273	        """
274	        evidence_chain = state.get("evidence_chain", [])
275	
276	        # Count new evidence
277	        new_evidence = 0
278	        for evidence in evidence_chain:
279	            # Get evidence ID (Evidence can be Pydantic model or dict)
280	            if isinstance(evidence, dict):
281	                eid = evidence.get("evidence_id", "")
282	            else:
283	                eid = getattr(evidence, "evidence_id", "")
284	
285	            if eid and eid not in self.saturation.evidence_hashes:
286	                self.saturation.evidence_hashes.add(eid)
287	                new_evidence += 1
288	
289	                # Track source type
290	                if isinstance(evidence, dict):
291	                    source_type = evidence.get("source_type", "unknown")
292	                else:
293	                    source_type = getattr(evidence, "source_type", "unknown")
294	                self.saturation.source_types_seen.add(source_type)
295	
296	        # Calculate novelty score
297	        total_evidence = len(evidence_chain)
298	        if total_evidence == 0:
299	            novelty = 1.0  # First iteration, all novel
300	        else:
301	            novelty = new_evidence / max(total_evidence, 1)
302	
303	        # Update history
304	        self.saturation.novelty_history.append(novelty)
305	        self.saturation.total_evidence = total_evidence
306	
307	        # Track consecutive low novelty
308	        if novelty < self.config.novelty_threshold:
309	            self.saturation.consecutive_low_novelty += 1
310	            logger.info(f"Low novelty iteration ({novelty:.2f} < {self.config.novelty_threshold}), streak: {self.saturation.consecutive_low_novelty}")
311	        else:
312	            self.saturation.consecutive_low_novelty = 0
313	
314	        # Record metrics
315	        quality = state.get("quality_metrics", {})
316	        critic_faithfulness = quality.get("faithfulness", 0) if isinstance(quality, dict) else getattr(quality, "faithfulness", 0)
317	        # FIX 23: Prefer Auditor's measured faithfulness for metrics
318	        auditor_faithfulness = state.get("post_hoc_faithfulness", 0)
319	        recorded_faithfulness = auditor_faithfulness if auditor_faithfulness > 0 else critic_faithfulness
320	
321	        metrics = IterationMetrics(
322	            iteration=current_iteration,
323	            timestamp=datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
324	            evidence_count=total_evidence,
325	            new_evidence_count=new_evidence,
326	            novelty_score=novelty,
327	            faithfulness=recorded_faithfulness,
328	            context_precision=quality.get("context_precision", 0) if isinstance(quality, dict) else getattr(quality, "context_precision", 0),
329	            answer_relevancy=quality.get("answer_relevancy", 0) if isinstance(quality, dict) else getattr(quality, "answer_relevancy", 0),
330	            gaps_identified=len(state.get("identified_gaps", [])),
331	            gaps_filled=state.get("gaps_filled", 0),
332	            source_types=list(self.saturation.source_types_seen),
333	        )
334	        self.metrics_history.append(metrics)
335	
336	        logger.info(f"Iteration {current_iteration}: novelty={novelty:.2f}, evidence={total_evidence}, new={new_evidence}")
337	
338	        return novelty
339	
340	    def prioritize_gaps(
341	        self,
342	        gaps: List[Dict[str, Any]]
343	    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
344	        """
345	        Prioritize gaps for filling based on importance and attempts.
346	
347	        Args:
348	            gaps: List of identified gaps
349	
350	        Returns:
351	            Sorted list of gaps to address
352	        """
353	        scored_gaps = []
354	
355	        for gap in gaps:
356	            gap_id = gap.get("gap_id", str(hash(str(gap))))
357	
358	            # Check if max attempts exceeded
359	            attempts = self.saturation.gap_fill_attempts.get(gap_id, 0)
360	            if attempts >= self.config.max_gap_fill_attempts:
361	                continue  # Skip this gap
362	
363	            # Score based on severity and attempts
364	            severity = gap.get("severity", "medium")
365	            severity_score = {"critical": 1.0, "high": 0.8, "medium": 0.5, "low": 0.2}.get(severity, 0.5)
366	
367	            # Penalize gaps that have been attempted multiple times
368	            attempt_penalty = attempts * 0.2
369	
370	            final_score = severity_score - attempt_penalty
371	
372	            scored_gaps.append({
373	                **gap,
374	                "priority_score": final_score,
375	                "attempts": attempts
376	            })
377	
378	        # Sort by priority score (highest first)
379	        scored_gaps.sort(key=lambda g: g["priority_score"], reverse=True)
380	
381	        return scored_gaps
382	
383	    def record_gap_attempt(self, gap_id: str):
384	        """Record an attempt to fill a gap."""
385	        self.saturation.gap_fill_attempts[gap_id] = self.saturation.gap_fill_attempts.get(gap_id, 0) + 1
386

396	            "execution_time_minutes": (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - self.start_time).total_seconds() / 60,

419	def analyze_gaps(
420	    state: Dict[str, Any],
421	    iteration_manager: IterationManager
422	) -> Dict[str, Any]:
423	    """
424	    Analyze gaps in the current research state.
425	
426	    Args:
427	        state: Current research state
428	        iteration_manager: Iteration manager instance
429	
430	    Returns:
431	        Gap analysis with prioritized gaps
432	    """
433	    identified_gaps = state.get("identified_gaps", [])
434	    evidence_chain = state.get("evidence_chain", [])
435	    sub_queries = state.get("sub_queries", [])
436	
437	    # Helper to safely get attribute from dict or Pydantic model
438	    def safe_get(obj, key, default=""):
439	        if isinstance(obj, dict):
440	            return obj.get(key, default)
441	        return getattr(obj, key, default)
442	
443	    # Analyze sub-query coverage
444	    covered_queries = set()
445	    for evidence in evidence_chain:
446	        query_id = safe_get(evidence, "sub_query_id", "")
447	        if query_id:
448	            covered_queries.add(query_id)
449	
450	    # Find uncovered sub-queries
451	    uncovered_queries = []
452	    for sq in sub_queries:
453	        query_id = safe_get(sq, "query_id", "")
454	        if query_id and query_id not in covered_queries:
455	            uncovered_queries.append({
456	                "gap_id": f"uncovered_{query_id}",
457	                "gap_type": "uncovered_query",
458	                "description": f"Sub-query not covered: {safe_get(sq, 'query_text', '')}",
459	                "severity": "high",
460	            })
461	
462	    # Combine all gaps
463	    all_gaps = identified_gaps + uncovered_queries
464	
465	    # Prioritize
466	    prioritized = iteration_manager.prioritize_gaps(all_gaps)
467	
468	    return {
469	        "total_gaps": len(all_gaps),
470	        "identified_gaps": len(identified_gaps),
471	        "uncovered_queries": len(uncovered_queries),
472	        "prioritized_gaps": prioritized[:5],  # Top 5 gaps
473	        "coverage_ratio": len(covered_queries) / max(len(sub_queries), 1),
474	    }

481	def check_evidence_sufficiency(
482	    state: Dict[str, Any],
483	    config: IterationConfig
484	) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
485	    """
486	    Check if evidence is sufficient.
487	
488	    Args:
489	        state: Current research state
490	        config: Iteration configuration
491	
492	    Returns:
493	        Tuple of (is_sufficient, reason)
494	    """
495	    evidence_chain = state.get("evidence_chain", [])
496	
497	    # Check minimum evidence count
498	    if len(evidence_chain) < config.min_evidence_count:
499	        return False, f"Insufficient evidence ({len(evidence_chain)} < {config.min_evidence_count})"
500	
501	    # Check source diversity
502	    source_types = set()
503	    for evidence in evidence_chain:
504	        if isinstance(evidence, dict):
505	            source_type = evidence.get("source_type", "unknown")
506	        else:
507	            source_type = getattr(evidence, "source_type", "unknown")
508	        source_types.add(source_type)
509	
510	    if len(source_types) < config.min_source_diversity:
511	        return False, f"Insufficient source diversity ({len(source_types)} < {config.min_source_diversity})"
512	
513	    return True, "Evidence sufficient"

516	def calculate_overall_progress(
517	    state: Dict[str, Any],
518	    iteration_manager: IterationManager
519	) -> float:
520	    """
521	    Calculate overall research progress (0-1).
522	
523	    Args:
524	        state: Current research state
525	        iteration_manager: Iteration manager
526	
527	    Returns:
528	        Progress score from 0 to 1
529	    """
530	    weights = {
531	        "iteration_progress": 0.2,
532	        "evidence_progress": 0.3,
533	        "quality_progress": 0.3,
534	        "gap_progress": 0.2,
535	    }
536	
537	    config = iteration_manager.config
538	    current_iter = state.get("iteration_count", 0)
539	
540	    # Iteration progress
541	    iter_progress = min(current_iter / config.max_iterations, 1.0)
542	
543	    # Evidence progress
544	    evidence_count = len(state.get("evidence_chain", []))
545	    evidence_progress = min(evidence_count / (config.min_evidence_count * 2), 1.0)
546	
547	    # Quality progress
548	    quality = state.get("quality_metrics", {})
549	    if isinstance(quality, dict):
550	        avg_quality = (
551	            quality.get("faithfulness", 0) +
552	            quality.get("context_precision", 0) +
553	            quality.get("answer_relevancy", 0)
554	        ) / 3
555	    else:
556	        avg_quality = (
557	            getattr(quality, "faithfulness", 0) +
558	            getattr(quality, "context_precision", 0) +
559	            getattr(quality, "answer_relevancy", 0)
560	        ) / 3
561	    quality_progress = min(avg_quality / 0.8, 1.0)  # 0.8 is target
562	
563	    # Gap progress (inverse - fewer gaps = more progress)
564	    gaps = state.get("identified_gaps", [])
565	    gap_progress = 1.0 - min(len(gaps) / 10, 1.0)
566	
567	    # Weighted sum
568	    progress = (
569	        weights["iteration_progress"] * iter_progress +
570	        weights["evidence_progress"] * evidence_progress +
571	        weights["quality_progress"] * quality_progress +
572	        weights["gap_progress"] * gap_progress
573	    )
574	
575	    return progress

66	class DynamicReplanner:
67	    """
68	    Detects situations requiring research re-planning and generates
69	    adaptive queries to address them.
70	
71	    Monitors for:
72	    1. Contradictions between evidence pieces
73	    2. Unexpected findings that open new research directions
74	    3. Critical gaps that need immediate attention
75	    4. Evidence saturation in current direction
76	
77	    When triggered, generates new queries focused on the issue.
78	    """
79	
80	    def __init__(
81	        self,
82	        max_replans: int = 3,
83	        contradiction_threshold: float = 0.7,
84	        unexpected_finding_threshold: float = 0.8,
85	        critical_gap_threshold: int = 3,
86	        max_adaptive_queries: int = 10,
87	        novelty_weight: float = 0.4,
88	        relevance_weight: float = 0.6,
89	    ):
90	        """
91	        Initialize the replanner.
92	
93	        Args:
94	            max_replans: Maximum re-plans allowed per vector
95	            contradiction_threshold: Score to flag as contradiction
96	            unexpected_finding_threshold: Score for unexpected findings
97	            critical_gap_threshold: Number of critical gaps to trigger
98	            max_adaptive_queries: Max queries to generate per replan
99	            novelty_weight: Weight for novelty in query scoring
100	            relevance_weight: Weight for relevance in query scoring
101	        """
102	        self.max_replans = max_replans
103	        self.contradiction_threshold = contradiction_threshold
104	        self.unexpected_finding_threshold = unexpected_finding_threshold
105	        self.critical_gap_threshold = critical_gap_threshold
106	        self.max_adaptive_queries = max_adaptive_queries
107	        self.novelty_weight = novelty_weight
108	        self.relevance_weight = relevance_weight
109	
110	        # Track replan count
111	        self._replan_count = 0
112	        self._replan_history: List[ReplanDecision] = []
113	
114	    @property
115	    def replan_count(self) -> int:
116	        """Number of replans performed."""
117	        return self._replan_count
118	
119	    def should_replan(self, state: Dict[str, Any]) -> ReplanDecision:
120	        """
121	        Determine if research direction should be changed.
122	
123	        Checks for various triggers in priority order:
124	        1. Contradictions (highest priority)
125	        2. Critical gaps
126	        3. Unexpected findings
127	        4. Evidence saturation
128	
129	        Args:
130	            state: Current research state
131	
132	        Returns:
133	            ReplanDecision with recommendation and adaptive queries
134	        """
135	        if self._replan_count >= self.max_replans:
136	            return ReplanDecision(
137	                should_replan=False,
138	                trigger=ReplanTrigger.NONE,
139	                reason=f"Maximum replans ({self.max_replans}) reached",
140	                confidence=1.0
141	            )
142	
143	        # Check for contradictions
144	        contradiction = self._detect_contradictions(state)
145	        if contradiction:
146	            logger.info(
147	                f"[REPLAN] Contradiction detected: {contradiction.topic} "
148	                f"(score: {contradiction.contradiction_score:.2f})"
149	            )
150	            queries = self._generate_contradiction_queries(contradiction, state)
151	            return ReplanDecision(
152	                should_replan=True,
153	                trigger=ReplanTrigger.CONTRADICTION,
154	                reason=f"Evidence contradiction on: {contradiction.topic}",
155	                confidence=contradiction.contradiction_score,
156	                suggested_focus=contradiction.topic,
157	                adaptive_queries=queries
158	            )
159	
160	        # Check for critical gaps
161	        critical_gaps = self._detect_critical_gaps(state)
162	        if critical_gaps:
163	            logger.info(
164	                f"[REPLAN] Critical gaps detected: {len(critical_gaps)} high-priority gaps"
165	            )
166	            queries = self._generate_gap_queries(critical_gaps, state)
167	            return ReplanDecision(
168	                should_replan=True,
169	                trigger=ReplanTrigger.CRITICAL_GAP,
170	                reason=f"{len(critical_gaps)} critical gaps requiring attention",
171	                confidence=0.8,
172	                suggested_focus=critical_gaps[0].get("description", "Critical research gap"),
173	                adaptive_queries=queries
174	            )
175	
176	        # Check for unexpected findings
177	        unexpected = self._detect_unexpected_findings(state)
178	        if unexpected:
179	            logger.info(
180	                f"[REPLAN] Unexpected finding: {unexpected.finding[:60]}... "
181	                f"(novelty: {unexpected.novelty_score:.2f})"
182	            )
183	            queries = unexpected.suggested_queries[:self.max_adaptive_queries]
184	            return ReplanDecision(
185	                should_replan=True,
186	                trigger=ReplanTrigger.UNEXPECTED_FINDING,
187	                reason=f"Unexpected finding warrants investigation: {unexpected.finding[:100]}",
188	                confidence=unexpected.novelty_score,
189	                suggested_focus=unexpected.finding,
190	                adaptive_queries=queries
191	            )
192	
193	        # No trigger found
194	        return ReplanDecision(
195	            should_replan=False,
196	            trigger=ReplanTrigger.NONE,
197	            reason="Research proceeding normally",
198	            confidence=1.0
199	        )

201	    def execute_replan(self, decision: ReplanDecision, state: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
202	        """
203	        Execute a replan by updating state with new direction.
204	
205	        Args:
206	            decision: The replan decision
207	            state: Current research state
208	
209	        Returns:
210	            Updated state with new queries and focus
211	        """
212	        self._replan_count += 1
213	        self._replan_history.append(decision)
214	
215	        # Add adaptive queries to sub_queries
216	        from src.orchestration.state import SubQuery
217	        new_queries = []
218	        for i, query_text in enumerate(decision.adaptive_queries):
219	            query = SubQuery(
220	                query_id=f"adaptive_{self._replan_count}_{i}",
221	                query_text=query_text,
222	                expected_data_type="factual",
223	                priority=1,  # High priority for adaptive queries
224	                search_keywords=query_text.split()[:5],
225	                domain_hints=[],
226	                status="pending"
227	            )
228	            new_queries.append(query)
229	
230	        existing_queries = state.get("sub_queries", [])
231	        state["sub_queries"] = new_queries + list(existing_queries)
232	
233	        # Log the replan
234	        logger.info(
235	            f"[REPLAN] Executed replan #{self._replan_count}: "
236	            f"trigger={decision.trigger.value}, "
237	            f"new_queries={len(decision.adaptive_queries)}, "
238	            f"focus={decision.suggested_focus}"
239	        )
240
